questions.question: Explain the three essential properties of the consensus problem.
expectedPoints: Termination: Every correct process eventually decides on a value
Integrity: Each process decides at most one value, and if it decides, the value was proposed by some process
Agreement: All correct processes that decide must agree on the same value

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Two-Phase Commit and Three-Phase Commit protocols, highlighting their advantages and limitations.
expectedPoints: 2PC structure: Prepare phase and Commit/Abort phase
3PC structure: Prepare, PreCommit, and Commit phases
2PC limitation: Blocking if coordinator fails after prepare phase
3PC advantage: Non-blocking through the PreCommit phase
Use cases: 2PC for distributed transactions, 3PC for higher availability requirements
Complexity trade-off: 3PC requires more message rounds but provides better availability
